model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The budget-aware optimizer changes helped substantially, which suggests the current MLP is still optimization-limited rather than overfitting. The next clean test is a width increase only: doubling the base width raises representational capacity without changing the training loop or adding complexity beyond a single scalar change.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The first width increase improved loss again, so the model still appears capacity-limited under the fixed-step regime. Adding one more hidden block is the next minimal architecture change to test whether extra depth compounds the gains from the wider MLP without disturbing the optimizer settings that are already working.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The larger, deeper MLP is improving, but with fixed 256 optimizer steps it still only sees a limited number of examples per run. Increasing batch size is the cleanest way to allocate more of the dataset to each verifier budget without changing the optimizer or architecture that are already trending in the right direction.","secondary_modes":["summaries","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The jump to batch size 512 improved loss sharply, which usually increases the stable learning-rate range as well. A modest LR increase is the next low-complexity move to convert the larger effective data budget into faster optimization within the same 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The model is now much larger and each fixed-step run processes far more examples, so the remaining gains are likely to come from controlling co-adaptation rather than adding more capacity. A small dropout rate is the simplest regularization test here and fits the existing MLP structure cleanly.","secondary_modes":["layout","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The dropout trial moved in the wrong direction, so the next step should restore the cleaner baseline behavior and continue exploiting the strongest trend so far: more examples processed per fixed verifier step. Raising batch size again is the most direct way to test whether the budget is still data-exposure limited.","secondary_modes":["caching","summaries","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest empirical trend so far is that fixed-step performance improves dramatically when each optimizer step covers more of the training set. Since batch 1024 produced the largest gain yet, the highest-value next experiment is to keep the successful optimizer and architecture unchanged and push data exposure further with batch 2048.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Batch scaling is still the dominant source of improvement, and the current batch size is now large enough that the previous learning rate may be conservative. The most targeted next step is to keep the winning architecture and data exposure fixed and increase LR modestly so the model uses the 256-step budget more aggressively.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"At batch 2048 with a wider, deeper MLP, the model is getting far more optimization signal per run than the original setting, and the recent LR change suggests the regime is close to well-tuned already. The next clean adjustment is to slightly relax weight decay so the larger model can fit the data more fully without changing the successful optimizer family or schedule.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The best gains so far came from giving the model more useful signal per step and then increasing capacity enough to absorb it. With batch 2048 now exposing much more of the dataset inside the fixed 256-step budget, the next plausible improvement is a moderate width increase so the MLP can exploit that larger effective training set without changing the optimizer regime again.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 4","old":"DEPTH = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current trajectory still rewards added capacity after the jump in effective data exposure, and the last width increase improved validation loss without touching the training dynamics. The next minimal architecture test is one additional hidden block, which preserves the now-working optimizer setup while checking whether the model remains depth-limited at this scale.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The revised schedule improved validation loss, which suggests the large-batch optimization regime can still use a slightly more aggressive step size. The cleanest follow-up is a modest LR increase while keeping the now-better warmup and full cosine decay unchanged.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The current regime is a very large-batch AdamW run with only 256 optimizer steps, so the default second-moment timescale may be too sluggish. Lowering beta2 is a focused way to make AdamW adapt faster to the early-to-mid training dynamics without disturbing the architecture, batch size, or improved schedule.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The beta2 reduction improved loss, which is a strong sign that the large-batch, short-horizon regime benefits from faster second-moment adaptation. The next logical step is to continue in that direction with a moderate additional beta2 decrease while keeping the rest of the now-effective setup unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2560","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The further beta2 reduction hurt, so the next candidate should restore the better optimizer setting and continue along the most consistently successful direction in this run: increasing examples processed per fixed step. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 3072","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 2560","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last batch-size increase produced another substantial improvement, which confirms that this run is still strongly limited by examples processed per fixed optimizer step.
